mnemonics: JVM's MAIN Job: Automatically Invokes the main() Thread
keyPoints: Every Java application has at least one thread, the main thread, which begins execution at the main() method.
The main thread is the parent of all other threads spawned in the program.
You can control the main thread like any other thread by obtaining a reference to it with Thread.currentThread().
commonMistakes: Assuming the main thread has finished when main() method exits, even though child threads are still running.
Attempting to start the main thread (it is already started by the JVM) which will throw an IllegalThreadStateException.
Forgetting that the main thread is non-daemon, so the JVM will wait for it to terminate before shutting down the application.
